agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Leave management module within an HR software system. Governs the full lifecycle of employee leave: accrual, requesting, approval, consumption, carryover, and forfeiture. This is a payroll-adjacent domain where correctness directly affects employee compensation and labor law compliance.","bounded_context":"Leave Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Leave Balance","definition":"The number of accrued, unconsumed leave days an employee currently holds. Must never be negative."},{"term":"Accrual","definition":"The process by which leave days are earned. Employees accrue 1.5 days per month worked."},{"term":"Leave Request","definition":"A formal submission by an employee to consume accrued leave days for a specified date range. Has a lifecycle: Submitted → Approved/Rejected."},{"term":"Medical Leave","definition":"A special category of leave that may be submitted retroactively (after the absence) provided a doctor's note is attached within 5 calendar days of the absence."},{"term":"Working Days","definition":"Business days excluding weekends and company-recognized holidays. Used to calculate the advance notice requirement."},{"term":"Advance Notice Period","definition":"The minimum 3 working days before the requested leave start date by which a standard (non-medical) leave request must be submitted."},{"term":"Carryover","definition":"The transfer of unused leave balance from one calendar year to the next, capped at 10 days."},{"term":"Forfeiture","definition":"The automatic loss of unused leave days exceeding the 10-day carryover cap at calendar year-end."},{"term":"Contract End Date","definition":"The date on which an employee's current employment contract terminates. Leave requests may not span across this boundary."},{"term":"Doctor's Note","definition":"A medical document that must accompany retroactive medical leave requests, serving as proof of legitimate medical absence."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Employees accrue 1.5 days of leave per month worked.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The accrual rate of 1.5 days/month is a company policy that HR leadership could change. The mechanism of accrual is structural, but the rate is configurable policy.","testable_assertion":"After an employee completes N full months of work, their total accrued leave equals N × 1.5 days (before any consumption)."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Leave balance must never go negative. Leave cannot be taken before it is accrued.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A negative balance means an employee consumed leave they never earned — this is a state corruption. The system would be accounting for time-off that was never owed, breaking the integrity of the leave ledger.","testable_assertion":"For any employee at any point in time: leave_balance >= 0 AND approved_leave_days <= total_accrued_days."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Standard (non-medical) leave requests must be submitted at least 3 working days before the requested leave start date.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 3-working-day advance notice is an operational policy. Management could decide to change this to 2 or 5 days without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"A non-medical leave request submitted fewer than 3 working days before its start date is rejected at submission time."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Medical leave may be submitted retroactively, up to 5 days after the absence, provided a doctor's note is attached.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 5-day window and the doctor's note requirement are policy decisions, not physical or data-integrity constraints.","testable_assertion":"A medical leave request submitted more than 5 days after the absence date is rejected. A medical leave request submitted within 5 days but without a doctor's note attached is rejected."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Only a manager may approve or reject a leave request.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Authorization integrity — if a non-manager could approve leave, the approval chain is corrupted. This is an access-control invariant.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to approve or reject a leave request by a non-manager role is denied by the system."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Rejected leave does not consume leave balance.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Deducting balance for leave that was denied would corrupt the leave ledger. Balance must only decrease for approved, taken leave.","testable_assertion":"When a leave request is rejected, the employee's leave balance remains unchanged from before the request."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"A leave request must not span across the employee's contract end date.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"An employee cannot be on company leave after their contract ends — the employment relationship no longer exists. Allowing this would create a legal and payroll inconsistency.","testable_assertion":"A leave request where the end date is on or after the employee's contract end date is rejected at submission time."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"At calendar year-end, unused leave exceeding 10 days is forfeited. Only up to 10 days carry over.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 10-day carryover cap is a company policy. Leadership could raise or lower it. The mechanism of carryover/forfeiture is structural, but the threshold is policy.","testable_assertion":"On January 1 of a new year, an employee's carried-over balance equals min(previous_year_remaining_balance, 10)."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An employee has worked 4 full months and taken no leave","when":"The system calculates their leave balance","then":"The balance is exactly 6.0 days (4 × 1.5)"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 2 days","when":"They submit a leave request for 3 days","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'insufficient leave balance'"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 5 days","when":"They submit a non-medical leave request with a start date 2 working days from today","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'insufficient advance notice — minimum 3 working days required'"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 5 days","when":"They submit a non-medical leave request with a start date 3 working days from today","then":"The request is accepted for manager review"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An employee was absent 4 days ago due to illness","when":"They submit a medical leave request today with a valid doctor's note attached","then":"The request is accepted for manager review despite being retroactive"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An employee was absent 6 days ago due to illness","when":"They submit a medical leave request today with a doctor's note","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'medical leave must be submitted within 5 days of absence'"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An employee was absent 3 days ago due to illness","when":"They submit a medical leave request today without a doctor's note","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'doctor's note required for retroactive medical leave'"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A manager reviews a pending leave request","when":"The manager rejects the request","then":"The employee's leave balance remains unchanged"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A manager approves a leave request for 3 days","when":"The approval is recorded","then":"The employee's leave balance is reduced by 3 days"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An employee's contract ends on March 15","when":"They submit a leave request for March 13–16","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'leave request cannot span beyond contract end date'"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An employee has 14 unused leave days on December 31","when":"The calendar year rolls over to January 1","then":"Their new balance is 10 days and 4 days are forfeited"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"An employee has 8 unused leave days on December 31","when":"The calendar year rolls over to January 1","then":"Their new balance is 8 days (all carried over, under the 10-day cap)"},{"id":"AC-13","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 0 days","when":"They submit any leave request","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'insufficient leave balance'"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Employee submits leave request for exactly the contract end date (last working day)","expected_behavior":"Should be allowed — the request does not span across the end date, it ends on or before it. The spec says 'cannot span across,' meaning the leave end date must be before the contract end date."},{"scenario":"Medical leave submitted on exactly day 5 after absence","expected_behavior":"Should be accepted — 'within 5 days' includes the 5th day (boundary inclusive)."},{"scenario":"Leave request submitted exactly 3 working days in advance with holidays in between","expected_behavior":"Working days calculation must exclude weekends and company holidays. If a public holiday falls within the 3-day window, it does not count as a working day."},{"scenario":"Employee accrual for partial months (e.g., mid-month hire or termination)","expected_behavior":"Spec says '1.5 days per month worked' — unclear if partial months accrue proportionally or require full month completion. Needs clarification."},{"scenario":"Carryover year-end processing when a leave request is pending (submitted, not yet approved)","expected_behavior":"Pending requests have not consumed balance (only approved requests do per BR-6). The full unconsumed balance should be subject to the carryover rule. If the pending request is later approved in the new year, it deducts from the new year's balance."},{"scenario":"Two overlapping leave requests submitted for the same dates","expected_behavior":"System should prevent double-booking the same dates. One request per date range — duplicates or overlaps should be rejected or flagged."},{"scenario":"Manager attempts to approve leave but employee's balance has changed since submission (e.g., another request was approved first)","expected_behavior":"Balance check must be re-validated at approval time, not just at submission. The non-negative balance invariant (BR-2) must hold at the moment of approval."},{"scenario":"Employee submits medical leave for a future date (not retroactive)","expected_behavior":"Should follow standard leave rules (3 working days advance notice) since the retroactive exception only applies to past absences."},{"scenario":"Contract end date falls on a weekend or holiday","expected_behavior":"The leave request's end date is compared against the contract end date regardless of whether it's a working day. The constraint is calendar-based, not working-day-based."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect accrual or carryover logic can lead to over- or under-compensation of employees. Forfeiture errors could expose the company to labor disputes or unjust enrichment claims. Negative balance bugs are effectively unearned paid time off — direct financial loss.","operational":"Leave management directly affects workforce planning. If advance notice rules are not enforced, teams may be understaffed without warning. Carryover bugs at year-end could cause mass balance corrections.","user":"Employees expect accurate, transparent leave balances. Incorrect rejections erode trust in the HR system. Retroactive medical leave is a sensitive flow — false rejections during illness cause significant employee dissatisfaction.","risk":"Labor law compliance risk if accrual, forfeiture, or medical leave rules are incorrectly implemented. Jurisdictional labor laws may impose additional constraints not captured in this spec (e.g., minimum carryover, medical leave rights). The 10-day forfeiture cap may conflict with local labor regulations in some jurisdictions.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Employees","impact":"Directly affected by balance accuracy, request submission UX, and forfeiture fairness. Incorrect balances or wrongful rejections undermine trust."},{"group":"Managers","impact":"Responsible for approval workflow. Need clear visibility into team leave schedules and employee balances to make informed decisions."},{"group":"HR / People Operations","impact":"Owns policy configuration (accrual rates, carryover caps). Needs audit trails for disputes. Handles year-end forfeiture communications."},{"group":"Payroll Team","impact":"Leave balances feed into payroll calculations. Errors cascade into incorrect pay, requiring costly corrections."},{"group":"Legal / Compliance","impact":"Forfeiture rules and medical leave policies must comply with applicable labor laws. Non-compliance creates litigation risk."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The spec is clear and internally consistent — no contradictions found. Confidence is not higher because several operationally critical details are unspecified: partial month accrual, the definition of 'working days' (which holidays?), whether the 5-day medical window is calendar or working days, re-validation timing at approval, and jurisdictional labor law compliance. These gaps are real but manageable with clarification.","high_confidence_areas":["Non-negative balance invariant is clearly stated and unambiguous","Carryover cap logic is straightforward","Rejected leave not consuming balance is explicit","Contract end date boundary constraint is clear"],"low_confidence_areas":["Partial month accrual policy (hire/termination mid-month)","Whether '5 days' for medical leave means calendar days or working days","Working days definition (which holidays are recognized)","Balance re-validation timing: at submission, at approval, or both","Jurisdictional labor law compatibility of the 10-day forfeiture rule"],"assumptions":["Accrual of 1.5 days requires a full month worked — partial months do not accrue","The 5-day retroactive window for medical leave is measured in calendar days","Leave balance is re-validated at both submission and approval time to preserve the non-negative invariant","Overlapping leave requests for the same dates are not permitted","The forfeiture rule applies universally regardless of jurisdiction"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Partial month accrual is undefined. If an employee starts on the 15th or their contract ends mid-month, does the 1.5-day accrual apply proportionally, or only for fully completed months? This significantly affects balance calculations for onboarding and offboarding employees.","affected_stakeholders":["Employees","Payroll Team","HR"],"options":["A: Accrue only for full months worked","B: Accrue proportionally (e.g., 15 days worked in a 30-day month = 0.75 days)","C: Accrue full 1.5 days if employee works any portion of the month"],"recommendation":"Option A (full months only) is simplest and most common in HR systems, but Option B is fairer to employees. Recommend clarifying with HR policy owner."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The '5 days' retroactive window for medical leave is not specified as calendar days or working days. This matters because a Friday absence with a weekend in between could mean day 5 lands on Wednesday (calendar) or the following Monday (working days).","affected_stakeholders":["Employees","HR"],"options":["A: 5 calendar days","B: 5 working days"],"recommendation":"Calendar days is more common for medical deadlines and simpler to compute. Recommend Option A unless HR specifies otherwise."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"No definition of 'working days' is provided — specifically which public holidays the system should recognize. This affects the 3-day advance notice calculation and potentially the medical leave window.","affected_stakeholders":["Employees","Managers","HR"],"options":["A: Use a configurable company holiday calendar","B: Use only weekdays (Mon-Fri), ignore public holidays","C: Use jurisdiction-specific statutory holidays"],"recommendation":"Option A — a configurable holiday calendar is the standard approach in HR systems and accommodates multi-region companies."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Leave management module within an HR software system. Governs the full lifecycle of employee leave: accrual, requesting, approval, consumption, carryover, and forfeiture. This is a payroll-adjacent domain where correctness directly affects employee compensation and labor law compliance.","bounded_context":"Leave Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Leave Balance","definition":"The number of accrued, unconsumed leave days an employee currently holds.
